title: Traits of the Avadhuta
summary: Description of the free, wanderer sage who lives without rules. Addresses feeling restricted by society, social pressure, and rule compliance stress.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “Clad in rejected clothes thrown on the streets, he follows a path free of right or wrong actions. He sits naked in an empty dwelling, absorbed in the pure stainless eternal one.” and develops toward “Where there is no knowledge, verses do not go there. I am involved in the world and I am renounced. I have spoken the ultimate truth.”

verses.sanskrit: रथ्याकर्पटविरचितकन्थः पुण्यापुण्यविवर्जितपन्थः। शून्यागारे तिष्ठति नग्नो शुद्धनिरञ्जनसमरसमग्नः।।१।।
verses.translation: Clad in rejected clothes thrown on the streets, he follows a path free of right or wrong actions. He sits naked in an empty dwelling, absorbed in the pure stainless eternal one.
Having a goal devoid of goals, with a skill devoid of skills, he knows only pure truth. How can an Avadhuta engage in arguments?

verses.sanskrit: आशापाशविबन्धनमुक्ताः शौचाचारविवर्जितयुक्ताः। एवं सर्वविवर्जितसन्त स्तत्त्वं शुद्धनिरञ्जनवन्तः।।३।।
verses.translation: Free from the chains of hope. Devoid of manners and purification. Renouncing everything, he becomes peaceful. He becomes the truth - pure and stainless.

verses.sanskrit: कथमिह देहविदेहविचारः कथमिह रागविरागविचारः। निर्मलनिश्चलगगनाकारं स्वयमिह तत्त्वं सहजाकारम्।।४।।
verses.translation: How can there be questions of embodiment or disembodiment? How can there be questions of attachment and detachment? He is like sky - pure and innocent. He himself is the natural truth.

verses.sanskrit: कथमिह तत्त्वं विन्दति यत्र रूपमरूपं कथमिह तत्र। गगनाकारः परमो यत्र विषयीकरणं कथमिह तत्र।।५।।
verses.translation: Where the truth is known, how can there be forms and formlessness. If the ultimate is like space, how can there be objectification?

verses.sanskrit: गगनाकारनिरन्तरहंस स्तत्त्वविशुद्धनिरञ्जनहंसः। एवं कथमिह भिन्नविभिन्नं बन्धविबन्धविकारविभिन्नम्।।६।।
verses.translation: The swan is eternal like space. The swan is clear and spotless truth. How can there be differences or liberation and divisions?

verses.sanskrit: केवलतत्त्वनिरन्तरसर्वं योगवियोगौ कथमिह गर्वम्। एवं परमनिरन्तरसर्व मेवं कथमिह सारविसारम्।।७।।
verses.translation: Everything is one eternal truth. How can there be union or separation or the pride that arises from that. In that everything is ultimately eternal. How can there be any substance or non-substance?

verses.sanskrit: केवलतत्त्वनिरञ्जनसर्वं गगनाकारनिरन्तरशुद्धम्। एवं कथमिह सङ्गविसङ्गं सत्यं कथमिह रङ्गविरङ्गम्।।८।।
verses.translation: Everything is one eternal truth. It is like space pure and eternal. How can there be association and dissociation? How can the relations and non-relations be the truth?

verses.sanskrit: योगवियोगै रहितो योगी भोगविभोगै रहितो भोगी। एवं चरति हि मन्दं मन्दं मनसा कल्पितसहजानन्दम्।।९।।
verses.translation: The Yogi is devoid of union or separation. The enjoyer is devoid of enjoyment or lack of it. He wanders leisurely. His mind is filled with natural bliss.

verses.sanskrit: बोधविबोधैः सततं युक्तो द्वैताद्वैतैः कथमिह मुक्तः सहजो विरजः कथमिह योगी शुद्धनिरञ्जनसमरसभोगी।।१०।।
verses.translation: One who is always in knowledge and ignorance, how can he be free from duality and nonduality? How is the Yogi naturally detached? - By becoming the enjoyer of pure stainless eternal bliss.

verses.sanskrit: भग्नाभग्नविवर्जितभग्नो लग्नालग्नविवर्जितलग्नः। एवं कथमिह सारविसारः समरसतत्त्वं गगनाकारः।।११।।
verses.translation: The destroyer is free from destruction and non-destruction. The auspicious is free from auspicious and inauspicious. How can there be substance or non-substance? The blissful truth is like space.

verses.sanskrit: सततं सर्वविवर्जितयुक्तः सर्वं तत्त्वविवर्जितमुक्तः। एवं कथमिह जीवितमरणं ध्यानाध्यानैः कथमिह करणम्।।१२।।
verses.translation: Forever attaining freedom from everything, liberated from everything, having known the truth, how can here be life and death? How can meditation or absence of it is of any consequence?

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रजालमिदं सर्वं यथा मरुमरीचिका। अखण्डितमनाकारो वर्तते केवलः शिवः।।१३।।
verses.translation: All existence is illusory like a mirage in desert. Undivided and formless only Consciousness exists.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मादौ मोक्षपर्यन्तं निरीहाः सर्वथा वयम्। कथं रागविरागैश्च कल्पयन्ति विपश्चितः।।१४।।
verses.translation: We’re completely indifferent to everything, from the right conduct to the attainment of liberation. How, then, could those who profess wisdom imagine either attachment or detachment?

verses.sanskrit: विन्दति विन्दति न हि न हि यत्र छन्दोलक्षणं न हि न हि तत्र। समरसमग्नो भावितपूतः प्रलपति तत्त्वं परमवधूतः।।१५।।
verses.translation: Where there is no knowledge, verses do not go there. I am involved in the world and I am renounced. I have spoken the ultimate truth.
